scenarios.clean: Normal record, all required fields populated, no mutations.
scenarios.within_feed_duplicate_winner: Last occurrence of a duplicated partner_member_id within a feed; should win in BR-601 last-record-wins dedup.
scenarios.within_feed_duplicate_loser: Earlier occurrence of a duplicated partner_member_id; loses to the later row in BR-601 dedup.
scenarios.missing_required_last_name: Last name field is empty — should fail BR-301 Required-tier validation and route to per-record quarantine (BR-302).
scenarios.invalid_required_dob: DOB field is empty — should fail BR-301 Required-tier validation.
scenarios.format_error_short_year: DOB rendered with 2-digit year (e.g., '4/12/85') — exercises date-format normalization in the DQ engine (A3).
scenarios.cross_partner_near_match: Same underlying truth identity present in both PARTNER_A and PARTNER_B feeds with deliberate mutations (name typo, address format diff). The Splink/DuckDB resolver (A4) must merge these via Tier 2 or queue them at Tier 3.
scenarios.deletion_fixture: Drives the BR-701..BR-704 demonstration: appears on PARTNER_A day 1, absent from PARTNER_A day 2 (deleted between snapshots), re-introduced on PARTNER_B day 2 with a name typo. The deletion ledger must suppress the re-introduction to SUPPRESSED_DELETED.
scenarios.tier3_ambiguity: Real-world coincidence: a PARTNER_B record shares first_name and ssn_last4 with a PARTNER_A truth but has different DOB + different last_name. Splink's match weight lands between the review and auto-merge thresholds (BR-101 Tier 3) — the pair routes to the review queue with the per-comparison breakdown so a human can decide MERGE vs DISTINCT.
